From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This template is being worked on by [WikiProject Christian liturgical year](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Christian_liturgical_year "Wikipedia:WikiProject Christian liturgical year").

## Masturbation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Masturbation")]

The inclusion of [Masturbation](/wiki/Masturbation "Masturbation") is likely in error. Possibly a misunderstanding of [Palm Sunday](/wiki/Palm_Sunday "Palm Sunday"). — Preceding [unsigned](/wiki/Wikipedia:Signatures "Wikipedia:Signatures") comment added by [Gekaap](/w/index.php?title=User:Gekaap&action=edit&redlink=1 "User:Gekaap (page does not exist)") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Gekaap "User talk:Gekaap") • [contribs](/wiki/Special:Contributions/Gekaap "Special:Contributions/Gekaap")) [15:25, 27 March 2016 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Gekaap-2016-03-27T15:25:00.000Z-Masturbation "9 years ago")[reply]

## Red links in template

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Red links in template")]

The article on [Epiphany](/wiki/Epiphany "Epiphany") covers the whole time, so I modified the Template to link to that, rather than redlinking to Epiphanytide. Feel free to change that if it doesn't work, though. I got rid of a couple other redlinks as well. I moved the "Easter (season)" article, which seemed like an awkward title, to [Eastertide](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide") so that what was already in the template worked. [Pre-Lent](/wiki/Pre-Lent "Pre-Lent"), which had also linked red, I made a redirect to [Septuagesima](/wiki/Septuagesima "Septuagesima"), which covers that time, not just the one Sunday. [Jonathunder](/wiki/User:Jonathunder "User:Jonathunder") 04:39, 2005 Mar 21 (UTC)

:   Thanks for that. I think there is a question about nomenclature for these seasons and feasts. There is no one agreed system. [Gareth Hughes](/wiki/User:Garzo "User:Garzo") 10:55, 21 Mar 2005 (UTC)

## Kingdomtide

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kingdomtide")]

As the linked article for [Kingdomtide](/wiki/Kingdomtide "Kingdomtide") indicates, it (a) was never generally observed in the western calendar, (b) was never co-terminous with Ordinary Time, and (c) has fallen into disuse. I am therefore going to remove it. [Vilĉjo](/wiki/User:Vilcxjo "User:Vilcxjo") 09:45, 17 May 2007 (UTC)
Yes, it is not "Western", but particular to some modern denominations. I agree. [Richardson mcphillips](/w/index.php?title=User:Richardson_mcphillips&action=edit&redlink=1 "User:Richardson mcphillips (page does not exist)") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Richardson_mcphillips "User talk:Richardson mcphillips")) [13:16, 31 March 2015 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Vilcxjo-2007-05-17T09:45:00.000Z-Kingdomtide "18 years ago")[reply]

## this template

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: this template")]

This template has some specific problems - what is cjames' Fast? the "Eastern" tradition does not have "Ordinary Time" or Septuagesima. What about Oriental, which includes the ancient non-Calchedonian Churches? E.g. The Fast of Nineveh is not found outside of the Oriental churches but in them is very important. I suggest this template be eliminated and the legitimate content put to "see also". --[Richardson mcphillips](/w/index.php?title=User:Richardson_mcphillips&action=edit&redlink=1 "User:Richardson mcphillips (page does not exist)") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Richardson_mcphillips "User talk:Richardson mcphillips")) [13:26, 31 March 2015 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Richardson_mcphillips-2015-03-31T13:26:00.000Z-this_template "10 years ago")[reply]

## Numbering of Ordinary TIme

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Numbering of Ordinary TIme")]

[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Template%3ALiturgical_year&type=revision&diff=992332708&oldid=992263012) 173.238.231.208, please find a reliable source which speaks of "Ordinary Time 1" and "Ordinary Time 2". I learned meanwhile from the version history that you added this numbering some time ago also without a source. However, it is just *Ordinary Time*.--[Medusahead](/wiki/User:Medusahead "User:Medusahead") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Medusahead "User talk:Medusahead")) [09:01, 7 December 2020 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Medusahead-2020-12-07T09:01:00.000Z-Numbering_of_Ordinary_TIme "5 years ago")[reply]

## Indentation of "Ordinary Time"

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Template_talk:Liturgical_year&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Indentation of \"Ordinary Time\"")]

In this table, why is "Ordinary Time" indented, in the list headers, under "Epiphany?" Logically, it should be at the same header-level as the rest—it's not a subset of Epiphany. [Isamuel](/w/index.php?title=User:Isamuel&action=edit&redlink=1 "User:Isamuel (page does not exist)") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Isamuel "User talk:Isamuel")) [21:11, 5 January 2021 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Isamuel-2021-01-05T21:11:00.000Z-Indentation_of_"Ordinary_Time" "5 years ago")[reply]

:   Different liturgical traditions use different names for different seasons, but the various traditions follow the same general outline. This template tries to show the general outline while also giving the more precise names (indented) found in specific traditions. Some Western traditions use "Ordinary Time" to designate the post-Epiphany and post-Pentecost seasons; hence it is indented under those items. [Jdcompguy](/wiki/User:Jdcompguy "User:Jdcompguy") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Jdcompguy "User talk:Jdcompguy")) [05:49, 6 January 2021 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Jdcompguy-2021-01-06T05:49:00.000Z-Isamuel-2021-01-05T21:11:00.000Z "5 years ago")[reply]

:   I get what you're saying, and I see now that there are also indented items under "Pentecost" in the same fashion, which I hadn't seen clearly before. But that just makes me ask about the indentation conventions here. Items indented under another item in a list seem like they should be "part" of that item, or "examples" of that item—"Pre-Christmas" is an example that satisfies this. But it's weird that with both Epiphany and Pentecost, the indented list items that follow are basically just chronologically \*after\* the thing they're indented under. Does this make sense? I'm not sure what the better solution is here and it seems like a minor point I'm sure, but it's sticking in my craw. Thanks for engaging with this. [Isamuel](/w/index.php?title=User:Isamuel&action=edit&redlink=1 "User:Isamuel (page does not exist)") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Isamuel "User talk:Isamuel")) [22:00, 7 January 2021 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Isamuel-2021-01-07T22:00:00.000Z-Isamuel-2021-01-05T21:11:00.000Z "5 years ago")[reply]

    :   The "Epiphany" and "Pentecost" items link to [Epiphany season](/wiki/Epiphany_season "Epiphany season") and [Pentecost season](/wiki/Pentecost_season "Pentecost season") respectively. In certain liturgical traditions, "Ordinary Time" is the name given to both "Epiphany season" and "Pentecost season." When you speak of Ordinary Time being chronologically after, you're probably thinking of the *feasts* of Epiphany and Pentecost, not the seasons. [Jdcompguy](/wiki/User:Jdcompguy "User:Jdcompguy") ([talk](/wiki/User_talk:Jdcompguy "User talk:Jdcompguy")) [00:36, 8 January 2021 (UTC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year#c-Jdcompguy-2021-01-08T00:36:00.000Z-Isamuel-2021-01-07T22:00:00.000Z "5 years ago")[reply]
